summary: An edge field targeting an embedded-locus kind resolves against that kind's nested members — embedded kinds join the by_kind target set instead of presenting as unmodeled

edit.description: assemble_by_kind (main.rs:1006) keys only kinds carrying a lock kind-fact row; an embedded kind carries none by design (SDK declarations.ts:211-213 — it reaches the lock solely through its host's templates column, which from_kind_fact_row already lifts). So graph::admissibility (graph.rs:157) reports an edge targeting it as unmodeled and its member set is empty even when the host's templates are declared. Key embedded kinds into by_kind off hosts' declared templates, their members' Features off nested_member rows and layout member-collection entries — exact assembly is code's call (depth rule).

tests.asserts: an edge field targeting an embedded kind declared via a host's templates passes admissibility and resolves by identity to a declared nested member; a dangling entry is a route finding; a target kind no host declares stays an admissibility finding

acceptance: The field report's repro greens: a relationship edge field targeting an embedded kind (their `serves` -> `domain`) with the host's templates in the lock passes admissibility and resolves by member identity; a dangling entry is a finding; all gates green.
notes: Field report (23e43db) re-verified at 5eba801; anchors re-set at ship audit 2f9e277. Blocker LAYOUT-RELATIONSHIP-EDGES shipped (fcaf08e): layout_edge_fields (drift.rs:690) enumerates edge fields off assembly facts — the shared enumeration seam is settled. The by_kind gap survives it: assemble_by_kind still keys only kind-fact-row kinds, so an embedded target stays unmodeled. Spec settled, no fork: a nested member is a full member with its own kind.

summary: A built-in kind with lock-declared clause rows gates on those rows whole — extension by spread fires, array-surgery removal holds; the severity-flip-only layer retires

edit.description: The gate (main.rs:590) and `schema` (main.rs:292/299) compose a built-in kind's contract via `compose::effective` — the embedded default with per-clause severity flips only, a layering rule builtins.md rejects ('no layering rules, no precedence table'; 'the built-ins are first-party instances of it, never a privileged form'). The SDK erases an expect binding on a built-in kind to ordinary clause rows (declarations.ts:460; `skillDefaultContract` is exported for the spread, claude-code.ts:8), yet those rows otherwise never gate: an appended clause silently drops, an omitted default clause silently still gates. Compose rows-are-the-contract at both call sites: rows present for the kind lift through the same reject-loud path custom kinds use (`compose::default_contract_from_rows`, c8b78c5); no rows falls back to the embedded default (`builtin_default_contract`, main.rs:50) — adoption rides the kind import (builtins.md). Exact wiring is code's call (depth rule).

`effective` (compose.rs:110) and its severity-flip unit tests retire with the layer — a symbol retirement in a surviving file. Its doc's 'the same tolerant read the rest of the lock takes over hand-editable state' (compose.rs:108) narrates a premise pipeline.md rejects (the lock is tool-written whole, never patched), and the retirement closes the scope cut c8b78c5's commit body named out loud: an out-of-vocabulary row naming a built-in kind was still inert on this path.
Module doc (contract.rs:6) cites `crate::compose::effective` — restate over the surviving composition in the same pass.
Module doc (schema.rs:6) cites `crate::compose::effective` — restate over the surviving composition in the same pass.

`builtin_skill_contract` (acceptance.rs:36) and the diagnostic-set fixtures assume the embedded default gates regardless of declared rows — update to the rows-are-the-contract composition and add the gate-level assertions.

tests.asserts: a clause row a built-in kind's expect binding declares gates its members — an appended constructor clause (c8b78c5's range/enumOf tier) fires; a default clause the declared array omits does not gate; a rowless built-in kind still gates on the embedded default; an out-of-vocabulary row naming a built-in kind is a load error, never inert

acceptance: An author who spreads a shipped default contract and appends a clause sees that clause fire on the kind's members; a clause the author's array omits no longer gates; a built-in kind with no declared rows gates on the embedded default exactly as today; an out-of-vocabulary row naming a built-in kind rejects loud; all gates green.
notes: Found+scoped at residue sweep e5a7077; anchors re-set at ship audit 2f9e277. Rejected alternative: keep the flip layer and admit rows as extra clauses — still a precedence table builtins.md rejects; rows-or-default is pipeline.md's 'one input shape, two sources; there is no third'. blockedBy is file-serialization only (shares src/main.rs with the edge chain per the pending-entry disjoint rule), no semantic dependency.

summary: A present-but-malformed lock declaration row is a load error in every row family — the tool-written lock admits no degrade-to-absent read; absence stays legitimate

edit.description: `family()` (drift.rs:2179, filter_map at 2183) and the per-family `from_table` lifts silently drop a present row that fails its lift, narrated as 'the hand-editable-lock tolerance the rest of the readers share' (drift.rs:1401, 2144, 2274, 2593, 2711) — a premise pipeline.md rejects (tool-written whole, never patched) and invariant 6 names a gap, not a license: a dropped satisfies row forges an unfilled-requirement finding, a dropped kind row unmodels members. A present-but-malformed row (wrong-typed column, missing required column, malformed array element) becomes a load error surfaced through `read_declarations`' existing miette::Result (drift.rs:2110); a missing lock, an absent family, or an absent optional column stays legitimate absence. `source_deps` (drift.rs:1402) takes the same discipline; the retired narration goes with the behavior. `layout_edge_fields` (drift.rs:690; filter_map at 693, new at fcaf08e) skips a present `fact = "edge"` row missing its `field` column the same way — one more site of the class, covered by the load-tier reject or made loud in place.

The lock-row column reads that degrade an out-of-vocabulary value to absent — the locus/format label reads (kind.rs:695, 723) and the KIND.md-era narration beside them (kind.rs:531) — reject loud the same way; the unit test asserting the degrade (kind.rs:1323) flips to asserting the error.

The `fact = "edge"` assembly-row read (main.rs:1184: 'a row missing a column is skipped, not errored') is the same class — a present edge row missing its column is a load error, never a silently absent edge.
The cases asserting a malformed row reads as silent absence flip to asserting the load error; add per-family malformed-present cases beside the existing legitimate-absence ones.

tests.asserts: each declaration row family rejects a present-but-malformed row (wrong-typed column, out-of-vocabulary label, missing required column) as a read_declarations error naming the family; a missing lock, absent family, or absent optional column still reads as legitimate absence

acceptance: A committed lock carrying one malformed declaration row fails check/emit/read verbs loud at load, naming the row family — never a silently narrowed verdict; a lockless harness and every legitimately absent family/column behave exactly as today; all gates green.
notes: Found+scoped at residue sweep e5a7077; anchors re-set at ship audit 2f9e277; layout_edge_fields site added at sweep 503de24. c8b78c5 set the pattern (ClauseRowError) at the predicate tier; this is the from_table tier beneath it. read_declarations already returns miette::Result, so the ripple stays inside drift/kind/main plus the row suite — callers keep their signatures. blockedBy is file-serialization only (shares main.rs/drift.rs with upstream entries), no semantic dependency.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper`, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`, still at 0.0.5 pending publish — SDK-VERSION-LOCKSTEP); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. Re-verified at 2f9e277 (ship audit): still no `.github/workflows/release.yml` (only `temper.yml`, a check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest (`temper-flume-harness`).

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
